item.title: Illegal immigrant top Dem fought to free turns up on voter rolls: 'mockery of our immigration laws'
item.description: Sead Cecunjanin registered to vote in Connecticut after falsely claiming citizenship, despite being under a final deportation order since 1997.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic Sen. Richard Blumenthal is facing scrutiny after new records showed an illegal immigrant whose release from ICE custody he championed was registered to vote in Connecticut.

"Cecunjanin has made a mockery of our immigration laws on several occasions for more than two decades," a Department of Homeland Security spokesperson told Fox News Digital of the illegal immigrant on Wednesday.

Records obtained through a public-records request by the West Hartford Republican Town Committee show Seyo Cecunjanin was registered to vote through the Connecticut DMV in 2018. The committee posted the records on X.

A DHS spokesperson told Fox News Digital the Biden administration released the Montenegrin national back into the United States after he returned from Serbia in 2024—even though he had been under a final deportation order since 1997.

EXCLUSIVE: DHS SEEKS CUSTODY OF ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT CHARGED WITH MURDER AFTER TRUMP DEPORTATION, BIDEN RELEASE

Cecunjanin first entered the U.S. illegally in 1997 using a fraudulent Dutch passport and was issued a final order of removal later that year. Despite that order remaining in effect for more than 25 years, he departed for Serbia in July 2024, returned about two weeks later, and was released back into the U.S. under the Biden administration before his June 20 arrest. 

Blumenthal and other Connecticut Democratic officials pushed for Cecunjanin's release last month, holding a press conference and later touting his release, crediting their letters and public advocacy.

"His courage and faith are a lesson to all of us on this 250th national birthday. I look forward to welcoming him home. ICE seizing and detaining him was cruel and unconscionable. He was never a flight risk, or a danger to anyone," Blumenthal said in a July 3 statement celebrating the restaurant owner's release. 

"I’ll support his efforts to gain permanent legal status and more. Welcome home, Seyo," he added.

Cindy Wolfe Boynton, communications director for the Connecticut secretary of the state’s office, told Fox News Digital that Cecunjanin never voted.

"Our records show that he registered to vote through the DMV in 2018. He was marked as an inactive voter in January 2023 after failing to respond to a routine National Change-of-Address canvass asking him to confirm his name and address," said Boynton. "An inactive voter remains on the voter rolls but cannot vote until they confirm or update their registration information, as required by state and federal law."

NEW JERSEY REMOVES 6,600 PEOPLE FROM VOTER ROLLS WHO WERE ELIGIBLE TO CAST A BALLOT FOR 2024 ELECTION

Critics quickly took to X to highlight the revelation, renewing scrutiny over Democrats' handling of election security.

"It happened again," Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn. wrote on X. 

"HOLY CRAP The illegal alien who Connecticut Senator Richard Blumenthal and other Democrat officials rallied to support after he was detained by ICE… IS REGISTERED TO VOTE IN CT despite not being a citizen!" popular conservative X account Libs of TikTok posted.

"WHOA: Illegal Alien That Democrats Defended from Deportation Is Registered Voter. Senator Blumenthal and Connecticut Democrats tried to protect him," posted conservative commentator Benny Johnson.

SEN MARK WARNER CALLED OUT IN ABC INTERVIEW FOR INACCURATE VOTER ID CLAIMS

Fox News Digital reached out to the West Hartford Republican Town Committee and Blumenthal's office for comment.

item.title: Miami Heat post, then unlist YouTube livestream for "LeBron Introductory Press Conference"
item.description: The Miami Heat allegedly posted then unlisted a "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference" livestream on their official YouTube account as theories fly.
item.content:encoded:
Welcome back to "As The LeBron Turns."

That's right, folks! Though he may try to deny that he doesn't want the attention to be about him and his latest decision in free agency, LeBron James sure has been the focal point of several stories regarding his future.

In fairness to King James, a lot of that press has been a result of how teams and media members have handled his pending move, but I digress.

Speaking of which, the Miami Heat may have accidentally tipped their hand regarding LeBron's landing spot, and it happened on YouTube of all places.

It appears someone on Miami's social media team dropped the ball, posting then immediately unlisting a scheduled livestream on the team's official YouTube account for July 27.

The title of that livestream? "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference."

GIANNIS ANTETOKOUNMPO ERA TO END IN MILWAUKEE AS BUCKS WILL SEND TWO-TIME MVP TO MIAMI HEAT FOR YOUNG CORE

Well, well, well. I don't think it takes a genius to crack that code.

To their credit, a Heat spokesperson told ESPN's Shams Charania that the link was only posted to prepare for a "possible" LeBron James signing.

Sure, Jan. Sure.

BOBBY PORTIS JR REVEALS THE SURPRISING REASON BEHIND HIS NEW MIAMI HEAT JERSEY NUMBER

So, now the whole world likely knows where LeBron will be taking his talents this fall, and they might even know the exact date he's set to make his decision.

While we wait and see if the prophecy becomes a reality, let's check in on Miami's director of YouTube strategy.

Brutal.

Look, I know whoever the previous YouTube strategist was may have just spoiled the biggest free agent signing of the offseason, but getting fired (allegedly) feels a little harsh.

In actuality, clicking the link provided shows that the job was listed on July 1, long before the livestream snafu, so the conspiracy theories will have to be put on the backburner for the time being.

However, if the livestream link was created just as a precaution for a "possible" LeBron signing, then why did the Heat pull it down so quickly?

I think we all know what that means.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

If that's the case, then South Florida will be home to both Lionel Messi and LeBron James, which is an insane amount of star power for one city.

Sorry, Dolphins fans, but you'll have to make due with Malik Willis and TuTu Atwell.

Tough luck, but you'll always have 1972.

item.title: Olivia Dunne fires back at MLB team over obnoxious comment, NFL WAG Gia Duddy sizzles & ESPN's bloodbath
item.description: Olivia Dunne fired back at Cleveland after Paul Skenes dominated the Guardians, while the Pat McAfee salary narrative swirls amid ESPN firings.
item.content:encoded:
Penultimate Hump Day of July AND we have NFL teams reporting for training camp today? How lucky are we?

It's all coming together, folks. I can smell it. We're grinding right now, but it's close to paying off. August begins next week. This Saturday marks the five-week countdown to our first college football game of the year.

Five weeks. I've got cartons of milk in the fridge older than that. Who has it better than us, boys and girls? Nobody.

Let's roll.

Welcome to a Hump Day Nightcaps — the one where Olivia Dunne tells the Cleveland Indians (yep) to kick rocks while Paul Skenes stuffs them in a locker.

What else? I've got plenty of fallout from ESPN's ... rough ... week, Gia Duddy checks in, and Charlie Woods is sounding more and more like his daddy on the course.

That's a good thing, by the way. Always a good thing to be like Tiger on the course. Not so much off of it.

Grab you a brick wall to run through after you hear this speech from John Harbaugh, and settle in for a Hump Day 'Cap!

Lordy, John. What was that? To be fair, Harbaugh hasn't really had to do an introductory speech in two decades, so he's understandably rusty.

But goodness ... give me something. Anything. Let's get these fellas fired up, coach! Football is back. That's the worst thing I had to sit through today, and I had an hour Zoom call on "content creation."

Oh well. Harbaugh and the Giants will be a story all year. Can't wait to see that team on the field.

But first, let's head out to the diamond with Olivia Dunne and the Pittsburgh Pirates, and get this call started.

For those who weren't locked in on this week's thrilling Pirates-Guardians game, Paul Skenes dominated over 7 innings, giving up one run and striking out 8 in a 7-1 win.

Some poor intern who runs Cleveland's account jumped the gun when the game was tied 1-1, calling out Livvy with a slick little caption.

Our girl made sure to fire back:

"Game was Dunne at 7-1 Pirates."

Don't come at MLB's First Lady and miss, Indians! Paul Skenes, by the way, appears to have his swagger back. After a rocky start to the summer, he's given up just five runs over his last three starts (3-0) and stuck out 19.

The "Paul Skenes has lost his stuff" narrative appears to have been greatly exaggerated. Weird how those things happen, right?

Speaking of narratives ... let's talk about this Pat McAfee nonsense that's starting to percolate in the wake of ESPN's summer housecleaning.

For those who missed it (don't know how that's possible if you read OutKick/Fox News), the World Wide (Woke) Leader laid off (fired) dozens of employees yesterday.

Ryan Clark was let go during NFL Live. Cam Newton was canned. Tom Pelissero was cut. It was a gutting.

Pat McAfee, meanwhile, is reportedly earning close to $30 million and in talks to double that salary pretty soon.

JJ Watt joined the "progrum" yesterday and ... addressed the elephant in the room:

"Slow, slow news day over there on the ESPN airwaves, huh?" Watt said to begin the interview. "I mean, besides the fact that you’re getting $100 million… Did they keep anybody? Or is everybody fired to pay you? I’m trying to figure that out."

This narrative that ESPN fired all these guys to pay McAfee is so stupid. The narrative that Pat McAfee's salary is ultimately what got Ryan Clark fired is even dumber.

Here's the bottom line: ESPN views Pat McAfee as an asset. The network has put him everywhere since the two partnered up a few years back. Everywhere. Not just football, either.

He was just at the Home Run Derby last week! An event, by the way, that wasn't on ESPN for the first time in three decades.

Pat McAfee didn't cost Ryan Clark his job. Ryan Clark lost his job because he brought nothing to the table for ESPN, beyond an (increasingly) occasional headache because he said something stupid.

ESPN chose to cut ties with a liability, and put more money into an asset. That's not a new or novel concept.

It's how businesses have been run for years.

OK, let's rapid-fire this Hump Day class into a big Hump Day night. First up? You know who ESPN absolutely should NOT have fired?

Karl Ravech.

Remember the old days at ESPN? The glory days of baseball when they had Ravech, Peter Gammons and Harold Reynolds on set? Mix in some John Kruk and Steve Phillips, and boy, was it an absolute party.

Now? ESPN doesn't care about baseball. They've decided to put all their eggs in the NBA basket, and continue to distance themselves from baseball. The firing of Karl Ravech — who'd been there for 33 (!!!) years — was disgusting.

But not surprising.

Next? You know what else is disgusting? This little moment from Charlie Woods' round earlier this week:

Taking a picture during someone's backswing — a kid's backswing — during a golf tournament is just the worst. God, these people are insufferable.

Personally, I think every golf course in America should adopt Augusta's no cellphone policy. I'm serious. It's, easily, the best rule in sports.

PS: How about the absolute disgust out from Charlie after that iron off the tee? Reminds me of this great Tiger moment from back in the day:

Man, I miss the Big Cat on the course. We had it so good.

OK, that's it for today. Good work, everyone. In honor of NFL camps officially opening today, here's infamous NFL Draft WAG Gia Duddy begging summer to stick around just a few weeks longer.

See you tomorrow.

Is McAfee to blame for Ryan Clark's firing? Email me at Zach.Dean@OutKick.com.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Hawley to introduce bill blocking lavish USPS executive bonuses over mail delays, losses
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley's bill would block USPS executive bonuses unless on-time delivery hits 95%, citing $25 billion in losses over three fiscal years.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX: Sen. Josh Hawley, R-Mo., is proposing legislation that would withhold bonuses for U.S. Postal Service executives, citing years of staggering financial losses for the agency and its overall on-time delivery record.

Hawley, who has launched an investigation into the Postal Service over its service record, will name the legislation the No Bonuses for Bad Service Act, Fox News Digital has learned.

The bill would prohibit the USPS Board of Governors from approving any additional compensation for the postmaster general and deputy postmaster general for a fiscal year in which the agency doesn't meet or exceed an on-time delivery rate of 95% for all mail products.

"While Missourians don’t receive their mail on time, the Postmaster General receives a hefty bonus each year," Hawley wrote in a statement to Fox News Digital. "I am introducing the No Bonuses for Bad Service Act to ensure postal service leadership isn’t receiving bonuses while Americans’ mail goes undelivered. It’s time for the Postmaster General to do his job and put Americans first."

SENATOR JOSH HAWLEY DEMANDS ANSWERS FROM MLB ON 'PATTERN OF DISCRIMINATION' OVER WARNINGS TO GIANTS PLAYERS

Over the past ten years, postmasters general have received more than $2 million in additional compensation, even as delivery performance declined and budget numbers got worse, according to Hawley's office.

Among the reasons cited by Hawley for the proposed bill are the agency's $25 billion in financial losses over the past three fiscal years, which have coincided with an increase in postage rates and a dip in on-time delivery.

During the last three years of Louis DeJoy's tenure as postmaster general, the Board of Governors approved more than $567,000 in bonuses, Hawley said. Current Postmaster General David Steiner, who assumed office in July 2025, has already received $170,000 in bonuses, Hawley said.

SENATOR JOSH HAWLEY DEMANDS ANSWERS FROM MLB ON 'PATTERN OF DISCRIMINATION' OVER WARNINGS TO GIANTS PLAYERS

When Hawley confronted Steiner about the additional compensation during a June 24 hearing, Steiner said he doesn't control the doling out of bonuses.

"I am absolutely astounded at this today, general … and, frankly, if things don’t get better, you ought to resign, because you’re not doing the job. You’re leaving your good postal workers out there. You’re hanging them out to dry. They don’t make near what you do. They’re working their butts off, they’re getting blamed and you’re getting the bonus. We have a big problem. Based on what I’ve seen today, you’re part of it," Hawley said at the time.

Residents in rural parts of Missouri sometimes wait weeks for their mail, Hawley said. In addition, a large pile of undelivered mail was found in April in a vacant lot in north St. Louis.

"The pattern is clear: As service has gotten worse, the postmaster general’s pay has not," Hawley said.

Hawley was already investigating the Postal Service, accusing Steiner of ignoring congressional oversight while demanding records on the agency's use of outside restructuring consultants.

Fox News Digital has reached out to the postmaster general's office for comment.

item.title: GOP blocks Schumer's effort to ground taxpayer funding for Trump's Qatari Air Force One
item.description: Senate Republicans blocked Chuck Schumer's bill to halt taxpayer funding for the new Air Force One Boeing 747-8 retrofit over nuclear concerns.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump’s new Air Force One will remain in service, for now, after Senate Republicans blocked an attempt to prevent taxpayer dollars from upgrading the aircraft.

Democrats have spent the last year sounding the alarm over the Boeing 747-8 luxury jet gifted to Trump by Qatar over the hefty price tag to retrofit the aircraft and the security risks that it could pose in serving as the main plane for the president.

After Trump’s surprising admission over the weekend about the jet, which was unveiled as the latest addition to the Air Force One fleet by the White House last month, Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., tried to halt any taxpayer funds from flowing to the project.

NEW AIR FORCE ONE TAKES FLIGHT AS TRUMP UNVEILS REVAMPED PRESIDENTIAL AIRCRAFT

The administration estimated that upgrading the aircraft, which already has a base value of $400 million, could cost up to $1 billion. 

"The luxury jet may have come wrapped in a bow for Donald Trump, but it came with a massive price tag for the American people," Schumer said on the Senate floor. "Trump is asking taxpayers to fork over an estimated $1 billion to retrofit, and max out his flying palace, so it can meet the security standards of the existing Air Force One fleet."

"That, of course, we already paid for," he continued. "Americans can't afford gas, can't afford groceries, can't afford their electric bill. But Trump thinks they can afford to fly him in style."

Schumer tried to forcefully pass his bill, Presidential Airlift Security Act, Wednesday afternoon, which would require that no Department of War funding may go to the purchase, modification, restoration or maintenance of an aircraft previously owned by a foreign government.

TRUMP SAYS HE'S IRAN'S 'NO. 1' TARGET AS RENEWED CONFLICT RAISES ASSASSINATION FEARS

And it comes after Trump, after pressure from the Secret Service, opted to fly back to the U.S. from Turkey in a different plane than the new Air Force One because of a lack of proper security enhancements, like countermeasures for heat-seeking missiles.

Trump told reporters on Sunday, when asked why the deterrence systems weren’t visible on the new plane, said that the plane, "has a lot of capability, but as I understand it, in about a month or so, they’re going to send it to have it be maxed out."

SENATE DEMS DRAW RARE LINE AGAINST MUST-PASS PENTAGON BILL

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed Trump, and told reporters on Monday that while the new Air Force One is "perfectly safe for the president’s travels," it was slated to receive additional upgrades and enhancements in the fall that would keep the plane on the ground for a month.

"During that time, the president will fly on the old Air Force One," she said.

Schumer’s gamble was promptly blocked by Sen. Deb Fischer, R-Neb., who argued that the legislation was more far reaching than just halting taxpayer dollars from upgrading the Qatari jet, and hamper the country’s capabilities in the face of a nuclear threat.

"Unfortunately, this legislation would also apply to a different aircraft, the Survivable Air Operations Center aircraft, or SAOC," Fischer said. "Those aircraft were actually purchased from Korean Air. So this legislation would hamper a crucial program that ensures presidential safety in the event of a nuclear war."

She argued that the SAOC played a crucial role in the nation’s planning in response to a nuclear threat — Nuclear Command, Control, and Communications, known as NC3 — and that Schumer’s move would "significantly harm" the country’s key nuclear deterrent.

"This is the plane that will command and control our nuclear weapons on America's worst day. We cannot play these political games with the NC three," Fischer said.

item.title: Likely Platner replacement's resurfaced posts on 'democracy' come back to bite him: 'Hypocritical socialist'
item.description: Troy Jackson's past posts championing democracy and voter participation clash with his path to the Maine Senate nomination without a single vote cast.
item.content:encoded:
Former Maine Senate President Troy Jackson, the presumed replacement to Graham Platner on the Democratic ticket for U.S. Senate, has a long history of emphasizing the importance of democracy despite being set to accept a nomination without a single vote being cast for him.

"I've always believed that our democracy is at its best when everyone has a chance to participate – whether I agree with you on an issue or not," Jackson posted on X in 2020 leading up to a Maine Democratic primary.

Jackson, a fifth-generation logger who ran in the Democratic primary for governor and finished third, has echoed that sentiment on many occasions, stressing the importance of voters being heard.

"The work of our democracy is one of constant engagement," Jackson posted on X in 2024. The same year he wrote, "Our democracy hinges on our ability to participate in free and fair elections, and our ability to accept the results – even when it doesn’t go our way."

5 THINGS TO KNOW ABOUT GRAHAM PLATNER ALLY POISED TO TAKE ON SUSAN COLLINS

"I believe in my heart that our state is at its best when everyone has a chance to make their voice heard and fully participate in our democracy," Jackson posted on X last November.

Jackson, who has also promoted the "No Kings" protests and railed against oligarchs, has faced criticism in recent weeks from his Republican opponents for jumping in the race to replace Platner, and presumably winning, via a process where delegates and party officials control the outcome rather than voters.

"Troy Jackson has no problem casting his principles aside when the process is rigged in his favor," Natalie Baldassarre, RNC national press secretary, told Fox News Digital. "He's just another hypocritical socialist who talks like a logger but will cut deals with D.C. elites to game the system and get his way."

"There are no kings in America, right @TroyJackson207?" Kristen Cianci, Republican National Committee Northeast regional comms director, posted on X this week. "Doesn’t seem to bother you when you’re the one being crowned."

FORMER PLATNER ALLY TROY JACKSON LOCKS DOWN DELEGATES AS TOP RIVALS FOR DEMOCRATIC SENATE NOMINATION DROP OUT

"Troy Jackson has a clear path to be the nominee for Senate, almost a week BEFORE the nominating convention," Maine Republican state Rep. Laurel Libby posted on X. "So much for no kings… "

Additionally, some Democrats in Maine have expressed frustration about the lack of democracy in the process to replace Platner, Politico reported this week.

"We were not under the impression that people were going to be already coming into this process predetermined," Platner supporting Maine Democratic voter Lindsey Harwath told the outlet about the process. "That seems very undemocratic. That seems like rigging a process before you’ve done it."

Fox News Digital reached out to the Jackson campaign for comment.

Jackson won the vast majority of the 500 slots that were up for grabs on Saturday and Sunday as Maine's 16 counties held two days of delegate selection for next weekend's state Democratic Party nominating convention to replace Platner.

While the delegates on Jackson's slate are not required to support him at next weekend's convention in Bangor, Maine, he is now considered all but certain to secure the nomination and face off in the midterm elections against Republican Sen. Susan Collins in what's expected to be a combative and expensive ballot box battle. It will be among a dozen races nationwide that will determine if the Democrats can win back the Senate majority. The GOP currently holds the chamber 53-47.

Fox News Digital’s Paul Steinhauser contributed to this report.

item.title: Minnesota city sued for demanding Christian preacher pay vendor fee to hold pro-life sign
item.description: A federal civil rights lawsuit alleges a Minnesota city forced a Christian preacher to buy a license before expressing his beliefs on public property.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX — A Minnesota municipality faces a federal civil rights lawsuit after police charged an open-air Christian preacher with criminal trespassing for standing in a public parking lot.

The federal lawsuit, filed Tuesday by the Center for American Liberty on behalf of Bryan Conwell, accused officials of placing an unconstitutional "price tag" on free speech by forcing a peaceful speaker to buy a commercial license.

"The First Amendment doesn't come with a price tag," Mark Trammell, CEO of the Center for American Liberty, said in a statement.

GOP LAWMAKERS URGE SUPREME COURT TO STRENGTHEN FIRST AMENDMENT PROTECTIONS FOR STREET PREACHERS

"Government officials cannot force Americans to buy a license before they peacefully express their beliefs on public property. If they can silence Bryan today, they can silence anyone tomorrow."

The lawsuit, filed against the City of Benson, Minnesota, claims city police and officials violated the constitutional rights of Conwell, an open-air street preacher and vocal advocate against abortion.

Fox News Digital reached out to the City of Benson for comment but did not immediately hear back.

Lawsuit documents obtained by Fox News Digital explain that Conwell regularly holds signs in a public place and waits silently, speaking only to those who approach him and engage in conversation. The lawsuit stipulates he "blocks no one, threatens no one, and sells nothing. He asks only to be left alone to speak."

YOU SHOULDN’T NEED A PERMIT TO PRAY IN YOUR OWN HOME — THE SUPREME COURT SHOULD AGREE

It continues to explain that when Conwell chose to stand in a city parking lot during a farmers market event, one that is free and open to the public, city officials and police asked that he leave or pay a $20 fee to speak. The lawsuit claims they asserted "exclusive use" of the public property.

When Conwell refused to pay a vendor fee for non-commercial speech, police cited him for criminal trespass July 9 and issued a second citation when he returned on July 16. Conwell now faces up to 90 days in jail and a $1,000 fine for each citation.

Officer interactions captured on video and referenced in the lawsuit show police warned Conwell that his legal woes would worsen if he continued. 

"The more you come back, the more citations you're going to get," an officer told him.

US MONITORING ‘CONCERNING’ UK PROSECUTION OF RETIRED PASTOR WHO PREACHED JOHN 3:16 NEAR HOSPITAL

Video provided to Fox News Digital by the Center for American Liberty shows Conwell speaking to police while holding a sign reading "Abolish Abortion to the Glory of God." In the clip, he can be heard questioning why his activism is a problem, noting he did the same thing the week prior.

"Well, legally I can be here, and that's the whole thing. And so, I can't be cited. So, unless there's a crime being committed, why am I being cited?" Conwell asks in the video.

The lawsuit also cites a conversation between Conwell and an officer, one who wrote him the ticket, in which the officer expresses confusion over enforcing the town's policy against protected speech.

"To be honest, man, I want clarification too," the officer told Conwell, according to court filings. "There are crimes of trespassing and crimes of disorderly conduct, but also you have your First Amendment right, so we've got to try to figure that out. ... I know what you're doing, I get it. I'm glad someone is out here doing it."

CLICK HERE FOR MORE COVERAGE OF MEDIA AND CULTURE

The lawsuit alleges these actions constitute severe First Amendment violations, including infringement on freedom of speech, the free exercise of religion, viewpoint discrimination and unconstitutional fees on speech.

Conwell’s lawsuit comes amid a broader push by lawmakers and legal groups to protect street preachers from police overreach. Earlier this month, congressional Republicans urged the U.S. Supreme Court to take up a similar case involving Richard Hershey, a Christian evangelist who was threatened with arrest in 2020 for handing out pamphlets on a public sidewalk in Louisiana.

item.title: 3M Open 2026 best bets: Five golfers who can upset Scottie Scheffler at TPC Twin Cities this week
item.description: Scottie Scheffler makes his 3M Open debut at TPC Twin Cities as golf betting picks target Hideki Matsuyama and longshots in this weak field.
item.content:encoded:
For most sports and golf fans, the PGA TOUR season effectively ended last week after The 154th Open Championship, golf's final major of the year. However, there are no weeks off for us golf-betting degenerates, and the money won at this week's 3M Open at TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota, is still green.

TPC Twin Cities is a classic Midwest birdie-fest designed by Arnold Palmer, where low scores are mandatory. With water hazards lurking on 15 holes, driving accuracy and precise iron play are crucial. While the bentgrass greens are gentle, spraying off the tee guarantees a fast ticket home.

Somehow, 3M convinced World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler to fly from England to Minnesota, where he will make his tournament debut. Maybe Scottie wants to break out of his slump; after all, he hasn't won since the first event of the season: The American Express. Or perhaps 3M just made Scheffler an offer he couldn't refuse.

RYAN FOX DENIES CAMERON YOUNG, SAM BURNS AT THE OPEN CHAMPIONSHIP AS AMERICAN GOLF TAKES ANOTHER HIT

Like Scottie, I've only hit one outright this season, and I'm hoping for better luck in Minnesota this week. Despite Scheffler cashing a top-five bet for me at last week's British Open, I still lost 2.52 units (u), bringing my 2026 PGA TOUR balance to -53.52u. All this means is that I'm due. Without further ado, here are my horses for the course for TPC Twin Cities.

3M Open 2026 bet slip

The following odds are based on my previous bets on the golfers listed below. Subject to change.

🇯🇵 Hideki Matsuyama +3604 at Polymarket (0.55u) and Top-10 with ties +295 at Kalshi (0.5u)

🇺🇸 Ben Kohles +7400 (0.27u) and Top-20 with ties +225 (0.5u), both at DraftKings

🇩🇰 Rasmus Neergaard-Petersen +7884 at Polymarket (0.25u) and Top-20 with ties +252 at Kalshi (0.5u)

🇿🇦 Casey Jarvis +8233 (0.24u) at Polymarket and Top-20 with ties +239 at DraftKings (0.5u)

🇦🇷 Emiliano Grillo +10000 at DraftKings (0.2u) and Top-20 with ties +310 at Kalshi (0.5u)

The bottom line is Hideki is by far the second-best player in this field and his odds should be below +2000. He has been all over the place with his driver this season. Still, he has gained strokes on the field in driving accuracy in three straight starts: U.S. Open (major), the Travelers Championship ("signature event") and The Open Championship (major).

Matsuyama’s TPC Twin Cities record is nothing to brag about — T7 in 2019, withdrew in 2022 and T30 in 2023 — but at least he has experience here. There are plenty of birdie opportunities at TPC Twin Cities, as long as you don’t hit into one of the many bodies of water, and these greens are easy. Hideki has gained strokes putting in five of his last six starts, per DataGolf.com, and three of the four majors this year (The Masters, PGA Championship, British Open).

Believe it or not, this winless jabroni is first in my model at Bet The Number. Granted, driving accuracy and iron play are my two most important skills for my 3M Open model, and Kohles leads the field in driving accuracy over the last 40 rounds and ranks second in Strokes Gained (SG): Approach. Yet, it’s still ridiculous to see him ahead of Scottie.

Nevertheless, Kohles ranks 19th in this field for SG: Putting over the last 12 rounds, per Bet The Number, which is his biggest weakness. He could’ve, should’ve won the 2026 John Deere Classic a few weeks ago but choked it away with a double bogey on the final hole to finish T3.

That said, I'm choosing to look at that as a good thing because he got into contention, and you need to be in it to win it. Lastly, a lot of longshots have won the 3M Open and Kohles’ game fits TPC Twin Cities, making anything above +6000 a "good number."

A prodigious driver, Rasmus has gained strokes off-the-tee in 12 consecutive starts, per DataGolf.com, including The Masters, PGA Championship and British Open. He’s made six straight cuts, with his best finish in a single-person event being a T9 at last week’s Open Championship.

Neergaard-Petersen has gotten in the mix at other high-profile events, such as a T3 at the 2025 DP World Tour Championship in November, followed by a win at the 2025 Crown Australian Open a month later.

OUTKICK SPORTS CARTOON OF THE DAY: RYAN CLARK'S LAST BIG ACT AT ESPN

According to Bet The Number, which only factors in PGA TOUR shot-link data, the Dane ranks 12th in this field in SG: Putting over the last 12 rounds. Bombers who have a hot putting week have won five of the seven 3M Opens, and Neergaard-Petersen fits that mold.

This is one of the most random golf bets I’ve ever made, which is saying a lot. I’ve never seen Jarvis play before, and he’s not even a full-time PGA TOUR pro. But I saw he had +5652 odds at Bookmaker at the time of writing, which is way off-market from the legal U.S. sportsbooks and prediction markets. That’s significant because Bookmaker is considered a "sharp sportsbook" for golf betting.

After seeing that Jarvis was steamed at Bookmaker, I wanted to figure out why. Surprisingly, the South African ranks second on the DP World Tour this year in stroke average and has two wins. Before you poo-poo his DP World Tour standing, know that those rankings include Rory McIlroy, Viktor Hovland, Patrick Reed and Tommy Fleetwood, among other full-time PGA TOUR pros.

Furthermore, Jarvis finished T21 at the Genesis Scottish Open two weeks ago, a co-sanctioned event between the PGA TOUR and DP World Tour, and T6 last week at The Open Championship. Jarvis is only 22-years-old, so maybe he can actually play. At this price, in this weak field, I’m willing to take a shot on him.

TPC Twin Cities is Grillo’s Happy Place. Here is his record in the 3M Open: T20 last year, T24 in 2024, T10 in 2023, T2 in 2022, missed cut in 2021 and T3 in 2020. The Argentinian is 12th on TOUR this season in driving accuracy and has gained strokes on the field in driving accuracy in his six appearances at TPC Twin Cities.

Grillo is a notoriously weak-to-inconsistent putter, but he’s gained strokes on the greens in five of his six 3M Opens and three straight starts heading into TPC Twin Cities, including at Shinnecock for the U.S. Open, which has one of the most difficult green complexes in the world.

As you can tell by my season-long record, it’s tough to win a golf tournament. However, Emiliano loves this course, and he should perform better than his odds suggest and at least finish in the top 20.

Mayo Cup season standings: 4,482nd with $5,146,536

Who cares? I'm dead in this contest, and I'd need the first place for this tournament to be worth four times as much to make any notable improvement in the standings. But, rather than be a coward and omit this section, I'll own up to my garbage season in golf betting and play all the holes.

_____________________________

Follow me on X @Geoffery-Clark, and check out my "OutKick Bets Podcast" for more betting content and random rants.

item.title: Jasmine Crockett roasted for claiming ‘racist history’ led World Cup fans to root against Argentina
item.description: Jasmine Crockett claimed World Cup fans rooted against Argentina due to its "racist history," sparking widespread mockery across social media.
item.content:encoded:
Progressive Rep. Jasmine Crockett, D-Texas, is garnering widespread mockery for her "delusional" claim that FIFA World Cup fans rooted against Argentina due to the country’s "racist history."

During a House Oversight and Government Reform Subcommittee hearing on Tuesday, Crockett remarked that the World Cup "captured the imaginations of the entire world when they saw all of these people of color that was playing for all these teams."

Addressing one of the witnesses testifying at the hearing, she said, "Ultimately, if you recall, the last two teams, it was Argentina and Spain, and it seemed like the vast majority of the world was rooting for Argentina or actually against Argentina. Right? And why was that? Just give me an explanation. If you could guess."

GROWING TREND OF HATING MEN BACKFIRES AS ANTI-MARRIAGE CULTURE LEAVES SINGLE WOMEN LOSING OUT

The witness replied, "Spain had the best team, maybe that’s why they were rooting for them." 

Crockett laughed and said, "There’s also a racist history that exists as it relates to Argentina."

Though subcommittee Chair Rep. Timothy Burchett, D-Tenn., quickly moved the hearing along, social media was not so quick to overlook Crockett’s claim.

Columnist Kelly Rae Robertson said, "Jasmine Crockett is insufferable. Another delusional take claiming a World Cup match was racist? Why is she still here — I thought she was leaving?"

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

The congresswoman recently lost her U.S. Senate primary to challenger James Talarico. Because she opted not to seek re-election to her House seat while running for the Senate, she is set to retire from Congress next January.

"I'm so confused as to why this is being discussed in a Congressional hearing lol," political commentator and women’s sports advocate Riley Gaines posted.

WATCH: SMITHSONIAN HISTORY MUSEUM DIRECTOR STUMBLES OVER BASIC QUESTIONS ON SEX: ‘I’M NOT SURE’

In another post, Gaines wrote, "Spain: Wins the World Cup That's it. That's literally it. Jasmine Crockett: ‘RACISM!!!!’"

"Rep. Jasmine Crockett is getting clowned nationwide," conservative commentator Eric Daugherty observed.

"WITNESS: Spain had the best team... CROCKETT: There's also a RACIST HISTORY! You can't make this up. She's too low IQ to be in the halls of Congress!" Daugherty wrote.

AMERICA BASHED AS 'DISGUSTING' FASCIST REGIME DURING PROGRESSIVE CALL WITH ABDUL EL-SAYED, CORI BUSH

Agustín Almada, an Argentine economist, appeared to take offense at the comment, posting on X, "This is a blatant lie. Argentina started to get rid of slavery in our war of independence from Spain!"

Spain defeated Argentina in the World Cup final in New Jersey on Sunday. President Donald Trump and FIFA President Gianni Infantino presented Spain its trophy.

item.title: Caitlin Clark creates WNBA ratings tug-of-war as CBS, NBC tout dueling viewership records
item.description: CBS and NBC both claim the most-watched WNBA game of 2026, but dueling announcements reveal how Caitlin Clark changed the discussion about league ratings.
item.content:encoded:
Caitlin Clark’s popularity has reached a new level.

It’s no longer noteworthy simply because she delivers another massive television audience. Now, major networks are simultaneously claiming that they carried the most-watched WNBA game of the season. Of course, both games included Clark and the Indiana Fever.

CAITLIN CLARK DECLINES WNBA 3-POINT CONTEST INVITATION, DEALING MAJOR BLOW TO ALL-STAR WEEKEND AND ESPN

CBS Sports announced Tuesday that the July 18 game between the Indiana Fever and New York Liberty averaged 2.579 million viewers and peaked at just over 3 million.

Caitlin Clark helps WNBA hit historic late-night cable number despite limited return in LA

The network called it the most-watched WNBA game on any network this season and the second-most-watched WNBA game on any network since 2000.

There’s just one issue.

NBC made essentially the same announcement six days earlier.

NBC said the Fever’s July 12 game against the Las Vegas Aces averaged a Total Audience Delivery of 2.64 million viewers across NBC and Peacock. The network called it the most-watched WNBA game of the 2026 season and the second-most-watched WNBA regular-season game since 2000.

So, which network actually carried the biggest WNBA game of the year?

Potentially both.

The difference comes down to how the audiences were measured and how the networks worded their respective claims.

CBS’s 2.579 million figure was based on Nielsen Big Data + Panel measurement. NBC’s 2.64 million figure was a combined Total Audience Delivery across NBC and Peacock, incorporating Nielsen’s television data and digital viewing measured by Adobe Analytics.

In other words, CBS is claiming the most-watched WNBA game on an individual television network. NBC is claiming the largest combined audience across its broadcast and streaming platforms.

The NBC total topped the CBS number by only 61,000 viewers, a difference of approximately 2.4%. But because Peacock viewers were included in NBC’s figure, the two numbers aren’t exactly apples-to-apples.

Sports Media Watch reported the NBC linear audience at 2.46 million, with the rest of the Total Audience Delivery coming from streaming measured by Adobe Analytics.

Neither network is necessarily wrong. They are measuring slightly different things and promoting the version that allows each to claim the top spot.

That’s what network PR departments do.

But the dueling claims reveal something far more important than which company gets to put "most-watched" on a graphic.

WNBA ALL-STAR CHARITY INITIATIVE SUPPORTS SPORTS GROUP THAT SAYS IT IS ‘MOVING BEYOND THE BINARY OF GIRL’

Clark and the Fever produced two audiences exceeding 2.5 million viewers in six days, on two different networks, against two different opponents, and on two different days of the week.

The July 12 Fever-Aces game, which was played on a Sunday, averaged 2.64 million viewers and peaked at 2.8 million, despite tipping off at 9 p.m. ET and ending after 11 p.m. Indiana won the game, 109-75.

Six days later, Fever-Liberty averaged 2.579 million and drew more than three million viewers at its peak on a Saturday night.

CBS now owns the two largest reported Nielsen-measured single-network WNBA audiences of the season. The previous leader was the June 6 matchup between the same two teams, which averaged 2.557 million viewers.

The rematch didn’t merely approach that audience. It surpassed it. That also means that Clark might have helped create another must-see WNBA rivalry. Her games against Angel Reese-led teams have always drawn well, but now it appears that fans like to watch her face the New York Liberty, too.

For the record, the only WNBA regular-season game to draw a larger audience since 2000 was the Fever’s 2025 season opener against Angel Reese and the Chicago Sky, which averaged 2.7 million viewers on ABC and peaked at 3.1 million.

The Fever’s grip on the modern WNBA ratings leaderboard extends far beyond the top three. Based on published national television audiences, Indiana has appeared in the 28 highest-rated WNBA regular-season games of the past 20 years.

The first game on the list that did not feature the Fever is the May 2024 matchup between the Los Angeles Sparks and Las Vegas Aces, which averaged 1.34 million viewers on ABC and is tied for 29th with a Fever-Mercury game from later that season.

No WNBA game from 2009 through 2023 reached even 1 million viewers, and Candace Parker’s highly anticipated 2008 debut drew 1.07 million, meaning none of those games threatens the 1.34 million cutoff.

CAITLIN CLARK KEEPS DELIVERING RECORD WNBA VIEWERSHIP AS FEVER TOP 1 MILLION CABLE VIEWERS YET AGAIN

Essentially, Clark and the Fever have overwhelmed the WNBA ratings chart to the point that the league’s biggest modern audiences are now competing against one another.

The biggest question surrounding a Caitlin Clark-driven television rating is no longer whether it set some kind of record.

It’s which network gets to claim it.

item.title: Dem’s cognitive decline crackdown wins rare conservative praise: ‘Extremely based’
item.description: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez's cognitive testing amendment for Congress drew bipartisan praise and criticism after being rejected by voice vote.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic Washington Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez’s push to confront cognitive decline in Congress is finding rare common ground among liberals and conservatives frustrated with Washington’s aging political class.

"In every job I've ever had, there's a simple expectation," the congresswoman said in a video posted to X on Monday. "You actually have to be able to do the work. Either you can do it or you can't. And Congress shouldn't be any different."

Gluesenkamp Perez proposed an amendment to the FY27 Legislative Branch Appropriations bill in May that would have required "the Office of Congressional Conduct to develop a standard for what constitutes conduct that does not reflect creditably upon the House, as it relates to a member of Congress' ability to perform the duties of office unimpeded by significant irreversible cognitive impairment." The amendment was ultimately rejected by voice vote.

WATCH: LAWMAKERS REVEAL WHERE THEY STAND ON CONGRESSIONAL TERM LIMITS AFTER RECENT HEALTH SCARES

The congresswoman maintains that the fight to rein in what she calls the "gerontocracy," a term meaning rule by the elderly, is not over. Reactions on social media indicate that she may have allies on the other side of the aisle.

"I agree with her, again," Daniel Miressi, a Republican running to represent Connecticut’s 4th Congressional District, said in response to Gluesenkamp Perez’s video.

"Mandatory cognitive testing to be a politician?" Crémieux, a popular anonymous right-wing X account, wrote. "Extremely based. We should've had this sooner."

UNLIKELY BIPARTISAN SENATE DUO SEEKS TO PERMANENTLY BAN EX-LAWMAKERS FROM LOBBYING

Some on the right lamented that Republicans weren’t focusing on issues like this.

"If only Republicans in swing districts were savvy enough to advocate for issues like this, banning congressional stock trading, etc. instead of screaming ‘stop deporting illegals,’" Breitbart News political editor Bradley Jaye posted in response to Gluesenkamp Perez’s video.

Gluesenkamp Perez has criticized the consequences of an aging political class — particularly lawmakers remaining in office despite cognitive decline — for some time. In 2025, she argued that members who were too old to hold office and refusing to retire damaged public confidence in Congress.

'THIS IS NOT NORMAL': AOC UNLOADS ON MCCONNELL'S PROLONGED ABSENCE

The debate has frequently cited the late Sen. Dianne Feinstein, whose health and reported cognitive decline became a source of concern for Democrats before her death in 2023.

Just as her proposal attracted bipartisan support, it invited criticism from both sides of the aisle as well.

"[Majority Democrats] just wants a younger generation of corporate stooges," Mason Pressler, a school board member and organizer for Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed, wrote on X.

Majority Democrats is a center-left Democratic organization that has promoted Gluesenkamp Perez’s effort to make sure elected officials are lucid.

Some prominent conservatives argued that the congresswoman’s proposal doesn’t go far enough.

"Ignore the fact that our entire fiscal future is in jeopardy due to entitlements, crushing future generations, focus on cognitive tests that might get a few geezers out of office," commentator Richard Hanania, referencing Social Security and Medicare, wrote. "Some fight against ‘gerontocracy.’"

Gluesenkamp Perez made headlines in 2024 when she called on President Joe Biden to drop out of the presidential election, citing concerns over his health and age following his widely panned debate performance in June of that year.

The congresswoman, notably, is not hostile to elderly people serving in Congress generally, only when they are mentally unfit to do so.

"You should have members of Congress who are navigating those questions of health care and the medical conditions older people face," she said in a 2025 interview. "That makes a stronger body."

Gluesenkamp Perez’s office did not respond to a request for comment when reached by Fox News Digital on Friday.

item.title: Democrat rejects Mamdani as member of party, accuses socialists of attempting to 'hijack' message
item.description: Josh Gottheimer says Zohran Mamdani is not a Democrat, accusing the democratic socialist of trying to hijack the party with anti-police views.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Josh Gottheimer, D-N.J., said Wednesday he does not consider New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani a Democrat, arguing the Democratic socialist – and others like him – are attempting to "hijack" the party and pull it away from its traditional values.

"He's not a Democrat, he's socialist, and we know that the whole socialist movement is attempting to hijack the Democratic Party and many of their values are not our values," Gottheimer said on "America's Newsroom."

"We know that he's against police and came out strong against law enforcement and [has] come out strongly against prisons in the United States, and they want to eliminate the United States Senate," he continued.

BILL MAHER ADMITS HE COULD VOTE FOR JD VANCE, WARNS 'COWARDLY' DEMOCRATS FACE SOCIALIST TAKEOVER

"They have lots of views which are not in line with Democratic values. They're very anti-American, anti-Democratic, anti-capitalist, so asking me to understand a lot of their thinking, I can't."

The New Jersey Democrat's remarks came as he criticized Mamdani's defense of his pledge to honor an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he were to visit New York City this year.

Despite making it a tent of his campaign, Mamdani later fell back on the push to arrest Netanyahu, admitting he lacks the authority to do so.

MAMDANI GOES BACK ON CAMPAIGN PROMISE, SAYS NYC CAN'T ARREST NETANYAHU

Mamdani instead called on the federal government to execute the ICC's arrest warrant if the Israeli leader pays a visit to the Big Apple.

"The mayor is clearly obsessed with this issue. He should probably be a little more obsessed with making sure that his city is running well and that he's picking up the trash and fixing the potholes," Gottheimer said.

"What shocks me about all of this is the double standard. We've got Iranian leadership that have been in New York. Obviously, Iran [is] a top enemy of the United States of America, the regime there [is] killing Americans, including soldiers... I don't understand. He doesn't go after them," he added.

The New York City mayor's office directed Fox News Digital to responses Mamdani made during an unrelated press conference Wednesday, in which he defended his push for Netanyahu's arrest.

"I made very clear, as I have before, that my interest in executing that warrant is not specific to Benjamin Netanyahu, but to anyone who they put a warrant towards for war crimes and crimes against humanity," he answered in part.

"And part of that is a reflection of a universal politics, and it is from a universal politics that I can look [at] October 7th and describe it for what it is, which is a horrific war crime. I can also look at Benjamin Netanyahu and see the way that he has orchestrated this genocide and also deem him to similarly be a war criminal, and I think, especially at a time when there are those in Washington who are discussing measures to take apart the International Criminal Court, what so many are looking for is a sense that there is, in fact, a rule of law across the—where there are crimes that are so grave that they offend all of humanity. And... I believe that we should hold those who perpetrate them accountable."

item.title: WATCH: Squad Democrat speaks out after teen found shot dead at husband’s Boston property
item.description: Yoseliani Marte, 18, was found dead from a gunshot wound inside a rental property owned by Rep. Ayanna Pressley's husband Conan Harris in Mattapan.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Ayanna Pressley, D-Mass., said she is "centering the victim" after an 18-year-old woman was found shot dead Saturday inside a Boston home owned by the congresswoman’s husband.

The victim’s family identified her as 18-year-old Yoseliani Marte and said she died as a result of domestic violence. Boston police have not publicly identified her, confirmed that characterization or announced an arrest.

"As someone who has done the work advocating, you know, for accountability and healing for survivors of all crimes — yeah, my heart is heavy," Pressley said.

She continued, "I'm just really centering the victim right now. And that's what we all should be doing."

FAMILY IDENTIFIES YOUNG WOMAN FOUND FATALLY SHOT IN HOME OWNED BY REP AYANNA PRESSLEY'S HUSBAND

The victim was found dead from a gunshot wound inside the home owned by Pressley’s husband Conan Harris, who she has been married to since 2014.

In a GoFundMe post, the family identified the victim and claimed that she died as a result of domestic violence. The mother also claimed that she allegedly knows who may be responsible, according to Boston 25 News.

"As the mother to an 18-year-old daughter, right now, excitedly planning to take her to college for her freshman year," Pressley explained.

BOSTON POLICE INVESTIGATE DEATH AT HOME OWNED BY REP AYANNA PRESSLEY'S HUSBAND

"I'm devastated for the impacted family."

Authorities have not yet publicly identified the victim, nor announced any arrests in relation to the death at the residence in Boston’s Mattapan neighborhood. Police reports obtained by Fox News Digital say the victim suffered from a single gunshot wound.

"This young girl, who we didn't know, 18 years old," Pressley said. "I’m sure had her own plans and dreams that have all been cut short."

A spokesperson for Pressley's office told Boston 25 News that neither the congresswoman nor her family were involved in the incident and that Pressley extends her deepest condolences to the family and loved ones impacted by the tragedy.

The home is a two-family residence that is a rental property where neither Pressley nor Harris live. It is listed for sale at $1.15 million.

BOSTON POLICE INVESTIGATE DEATH AT HOME OWNED BY REP AYANNA PRESSLEY'S HUSBAND

It is still not clear at this time whether Marte lived at the home where she was found dead.

The mother also told Boston 25 News that she is focused on getting justice for her daughter and that "everything’s gonna come out," and "it’s all going to come to light."

The manner of death remains under investigation, Boston police said.

item.title: France becomes first EU nation to ban social media for children under 15
item.description: France banned social media for children under 15, but the age-verification rollout raises major questions about enforcement and privacy compliance.
item.content:encoded:
France has become the first country in the European Union to ban children under 15 from using social media after lawmakers approved sweeping legislation requiring platforms to block young users through age-verification systems, citing growing concerns over children's mental health and online safety.

"I had committed to it, and now it’s been voted on: social media will be banned for those under 15 starting back-to-school," French President Emmanuel Macron said in a Tuesday X post.

"Thanks to the parliamentarians," he added. "It’s up to the Constitutional Council to rule, then on to action to make this measure concrete and protect our children online."

UK TO BAN TIKTOK, YOUTUBE, OTHER SOCIAL MEDIA APPS FOR CHILDREN UNDER 16, STARMER SAYS

CNN reported Tuesday that France joins a growing list of countries moving to restrict children's access to social media, with Australia becoming the first nation to ban social media for users under 16 last year. The United Kingdom plans to introduce similar rules next year.

Spain, Greece and Denmark are also considering minimum age requirements. 

CNN reported that the French measure passed with overwhelming support in Parliament and now heads to Macron for his signature.

Australia requires platforms to take reasonable measures to keep children below the minimum age from creating or maintaining accounts, while French officials have pledged to introduce age-verification systems to enforce the legislation, according to the report.

META MEDICARE SCAM ADS TARGETING SENIORS FACE SCRUTINY

BBC reported that under the rollout plan, the law will first bar children under 15 from creating new social media accounts beginning in September, with age verification required for all new users.

Starting in January 2027, the requirement will expand to existing accounts, meaning all users in France will need to verify they are at least 15. According to the BBC, platforms will be required to use age-verification technology approved by France's privacy regulator.

USHA VANCE BACKS BOOKS OVER PHONES AS MORE SCHOOLS IMPLEMENT NO-CELL POLICIES

item.title: Vacation hot spot faces new CDC travel warning as outbreak cases continue climbing
item.description: The CDC issued a Level 2 Travel Health Notice for Costa Rica's Guanacaste Province after chikungunya cases surfaced near popular Playa Langosta. Travelers were told to take precautions.
item.content:encoded:
Americans traveling to Costa Rica are being urged to take extra precautions after U.S. health officials issued a new travel warning over an outbreak of the mosquito-borne virus chikungunya.

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recently issued a Level 2 Travel Health Notice for Costa Rica's Guanacaste Province, where most cases have been reported in the popular beach destination of Playa Langosta.

Known for its beaches, surfing and hiking, Guanacaste is one of Costa Rica's most popular tourist regions.

STOMACH BUGS SURGING AS DOCTORS SAY CERTAIN TRAVEL ESSENTIALS COULD SAVE YOUR TRIP

The agency is advising travelers to protect themselves against mosquito bites while visiting the area.

Chikungunya is spread through mosquito bites; it typically causes fever and severe joint pain, the CDC said in the travel notice. 

Symptoms usually begin three to seven days after an infected mosquito bite and may also include headache, muscle pain, joint swelling and rash.

While most people recover within a week, "some can have severe joint pain for months to years following acute illness," the CDC warns.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR OUR LIFESTYLE NEWSLETTER

There is no specific treatment for chikungunya, according to the agency. 

Prevention is the best way to avoid infection.

The CDC recommends using insect repellent, wearing long-sleeved shirts and pants and staying in accommodations with air conditioning or window screens. Vaccinations are also recommended for eligible travelers visiting an outbreak area.

Officials have confirmed four cases and identified another 17 probable cases linked to the outbreak, according to a notice from Costa Rica's Ministry of Health. Both Costa Rican residents and foreign visitors are among the cases under investigation.

Specialized teams have fumigated approximately 5,000 homes and eliminated mosquito breeding sites in an effort to contain the outbreak, the notice stated.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE LIFESTYLE STORIES

The CDC also advises pregnant women to reconsider traveling to the affected area, particularly if they're close to delivering their baby, because the virus can be passed to a newborn around the time of birth.

Travelers who develop fever, joint pain, headache, muscle pain, joint swelling or a rash during or after their trip should seek medical care, the agency said.

Fox News Digital reached out to the CDC for comment.

The advisory in Costa Rica is the latest in a series of chikungunya-related travel alerts.

Fox News Digital previously reported that the CDC issued a Level 2 Travel Health Notice for Bolivia earlier this year after a chikungunya outbreak in the country's Santa Cruz and Cochabamba departments.

The agency also issued Level 2 advisories for the Seychelles, a group of islands in the Indian Ocean, as well as Cuba, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and China's Guangdong Province after chikungunya outbreaks.

Ashley DiMella of Fox News Digital contributed reporting.

item.title: Accused murderer arrested while trying to board cruise ship in Florida, deputies say
item.description: Six cruise passengers headed to Port Canaveral landed in jail after Brevard County deputies found outstanding warrants and probation violations.
item.content:encoded:
A group of would-be cruisers hoping to sail the high seas instead landed at what a Florida sheriff dubbed his "Iron Bar Lodge" after deputies encountered six passengers with outstanding warrants or alleged probation violations at Port Canaveral.

"Oh Ship!!" Brevard County Sheriff Wayne Ivey wrote in a social media post recounting the busy day for deputies at one of the world’s busiest cruise ports.

The most serious case involved Martell Murray, 24, of Georgia, who was taken into custody on an out-of-state fugitive warrant alleging he violated conditions of his release in connection with pending murder charges, according to the sheriff’s office and Brevard County jail records.

'AMERICA'S MOST WANTED' FUGITIVE CAPTURED ON SAILBOAT OFF NJ COAST AFTER 20 YEARS ON THE RUN

Authorities said the warrant stemmed from an alleged violation tied to an existing murder case. The sheriff’s office did not identify the victim, disclose the jurisdiction handling the case or explain which release condition Murray had allegedly violated.

Murray’s getaway was sunk alongside his traveling companion, Kenderia Burney, 24, who was wanted on a violation-of-probation warrant, Ivey said.

DITCHING HIS 'WHIZZINATOR' IN A PATROL CAR WAS FLORIDA MAN'S SECOND BAD DECISION OF DAY

"Luckily these two still got to vacation together at the Lodge even though they got accommodations with separate sleeping quarters," Ivey quipped.

Both Murray and Burney were booked into the Brevard County Jail on July 17 and remained in custody without bond as of Wednesday, jail records showed. Murray was booked as an out-of-state fugitive, while Burney was being held on an out-of-county warrant.

GOT A TIP?

The deputies’ catch of the day did not end there.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

Veronica Hamlet, 38, of Tampa, was arrested on a failure-to-appear warrant, according to the sheriff. Brevard County jail records listed her charge as an out-of-county warrant and showed that she was released the following day after posting $1,000 bond.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

James Otha Brewton, 66, of Allendale, South Carolina, was also taken into custody on a violation-of-probation warrant, Ivey said. Jail records showed Brewton was booked on an out-of-county warrant and released July 18 after posting $500 bond.

"It’s smooth sailing from there," the sheriff said.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE U.S. NEWS

GET BREAKING NEWS ALERTS

Two additional passengers also found trouble waiting for them after they returned from their cruise.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

Deputies stopped the unnamed travelers as they disembarked and determined that both were on felony probation but allegedly had left the country without receiving permission from their probation officers, Ivey said.

The sheriff said the two would be enjoying stays at their respective "lodges" once probation officials completed the required paperwork.

SEND US A TIP

Authorities did not identify those passengers or disclose the crimes for which they were on probation.

The sheriff’s office did not identify the ships or cruise lines connected to the six passengers.

"Whether you’re from Florida or Alaska, if you come to Brevard County and break the law…or in this case have a warrant…you better believe that we will lock you up!!" he said.

FOLLOW US ON X

item.title: Italy shuts down effort linked to Gaza flotilla group to track Jewish, Israeli tourists
item.description: An anonymous online form asked the public to report locations of Israeli tourists and Italian Jews, drawing comparisons to fascist-era persecution.
item.content:encoded:
Italian authorities shut down an anonymous online effort that asked the public to report the locations of Israeli tourists, Italian Jews and businesses believed to have ties to Zionism, drawing condemnation from senior government officials and Jewish leaders who compared the initiative to fascist-era persecution.

"I think we’re going back to the 1930s, to hunting Jews," Walker Meghnagi, president of the Jewish Community of Milan, said after the Google form began circulating on social media and messaging platforms.

The questionnaire said it was intended to "map Zionist tourism," property purchases and what it described as "Zionist neo-colonization" in Italy, according to reports. It asked users to identify Jews, Israelis and people believed to be connected to Zionism at hotels, vacation rentals, businesses and other public places.

ISRAEL, JEWS TARGETED WORLDWIDE AS WELL-FUNDED LEFTIST, ISLAMIST GROUPS JOIN FOR ‘NAKBA 78’ PROTESTS

"The online mapping of so-called 'Zionist tourism,' rightly denounced by the president of the Jewish Community of Milan, Walker Meghnagi, is an aberrant initiative, in direct violation of fundamental human rights and the constitutional principles on which our nation is founded," Italian Senate President Ignazio La Russa wrote in a statement, as translated to English.

"No form of cataloguing of persons on the basis of their religious belief or their connection with Israel is acceptable or justifiable. Behind all this lies a profound anti-Semitism that must be unanimously, firmly, and determinedly rejected," he added. "We cannot remain motionless in the face of such serious facts that recall the ghosts of the past. To the Jewish Community all my closeness."

The form was removed following the intervention of Gen. Pasquale Angelosanto, Italy’s national coordinator for the fight against antisemitism, according to Italian and Israeli media reports.

'NOT ON OUR WATCH': GLOBAL LAW ENFORCEMENT LEADERS UNITE IN POLAND AGAINST HATE

"This form is intended to record cases of Zionist tourism, property purchases, and, more generally, Zionist neo-colonization in Italy," the questionnaire stated.

It said the information collected would be received by the Global Sumud Movement, an activist network associated with flotilla efforts to reach the Gaza Strip, and would remain confidential. Respondents were asked to provide sources so the reports could be verified and used to coordinate unspecified "initiatives."

The Global Sumud Movement denied commissioning the form or knowing about it before the controversy erupted, according to The Jerusalem Post.

"Zionism is one of the issues we work on, but we do not hunt Jews, and we know nothing about this questionnaire," the group told the Italian newspaper La Repubblica. "We did not commission it and had not even seen it before it was taken offline."

EXILED MUSLIM SCHOLAR WARNS FAR-LEFT–ISLAMIST ALLIANCE BEHIND ANTI-ISRAEL PROTESTS ECHOES IRAN’S RISE

The organization said it learned about the questionnaire through news reports and reserved the right to take further action, including issuing a formal statement.

According to La Repubblica, later sections of the form asked respondents to name locations where Jewish or Israeli visitors had been seen and to indicate whether local organizations or activist groups were already "managing the phenomenon."

Meghnagi warned that collecting and circulating such information could encourage extremists to target Jews and Israelis.

"What is happening today shows that there are unbalanced individuals out there, and if this kind of message spreads widely enough, you are certain to find someone willing to act on it," he said. "For this reason, mapping where Jews are is extremely serious."

Alessandro Morelli, an Italian deputy secretary of state, ripped the form as "abhorrent" and said those responsible should not go unpunished.

item.title: Mamdani sets internet ablaze with video explaining cave on plan to arrest Netanyahu: 'What a joke'
item.description: Zohran Mamdani admitted New York City cannot arrest Benjamin Netanyahu despite his campaign promises, drawing swift mockery from conservatives.
item.content:encoded:
New York City socialist mayor Zohran Mamdani was widely mocked by conservatives on social media Tuesday after announcing that one of his campaign platforms, arresting Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, isn’t a tool he has at his disposal.

In a video post on X that was viewed over 36 million times, Mamdani called Netanyahu a "war criminal" while conceding that New York City could not enforce the International Criminal Court's warrant for the Israeli leader’s arrest on its own despite the mayor saying he had looked at exploring avenues to do so.

Mamdani has often said on the campaign trail and as mayor that he wanted to arrest Netanyahu over what the mayor calls a "genocide" in Gaza.

While Mamdani’s post was well received by many on the political left, conservatives on social media quickly mocked the mayor over the situation while taking issue with his determination to enforce the ICC warrant, which the United States government does not recognize.

MAMDANI’S ‘DERANGED’ ISRAEL TAKE RESURFACES AS HE TARGETS NETANYAHU

"Trying to find where Mamdani called for the arrest of the Hamas terrorists who plotted the October 7th attacks," Arkansas Republican Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders posted on X. "Disgusting and Disgraceful."

"This may be the most horrific package of lies against the State of Israel ever promoted by a government official," former U.S. Ambassador to Israel David M. Friedman posted on X. "Even Hamas does not lie to this extent! It is despicable and entirely unbecoming a mayor of New York City. To those who voted for this Jew-hater, shame on you."

"Zohran Mamdani calling Benjamin Netanyahu a war criminal is utterly ridiculous," Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., posted on X. "The same crowd that rushes to condemn Israel never has much to say about the terrorists who started the war, massacred civilians, raped women, kidnapped children, and still hide behind their own people. Mamdani wants the headline that comes from attacking Netanyahu, not the consistency that comes with confronting Hamas."

MAMDANI CALLS OUT DEMOCRATIC PARTY AS 'WELL-PAID CONSULTANTS AND BIG MONEY INTERESTS'

"The mayor of New York is just straight up promoting antisemitic blood libel courtesy of Hamas propaganda from an official government social media account tonight," New York City Republican Councilwoman Vickie Paladino posted on X.

"As I was saying… he doesn’t have the authority," US Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz posted on X. "More political theater."

"I had a dead homeless body in front of my apartment, but the mayor’s focused on playing Model UN," Sam Raus, David Boaz Resident Writing Fellow at Young Voices, posted on X. "What a joke."

"Zohran Mamdani admitted tonight that he has zero legal right to arrest Netanyahu," political commentator Eyal Yakoby posted on X. "Like everything that he does, it was all just an act for clicks on social media."

Although Mamdani acknowledged he could not arrest Netanyahu as mayor, he called on the federal government to execute an International Criminal Court's arrest warrant if the Israeli leader visits New York.

"I want to be equally clear: Benjamin Netanyahu is not welcome in New York City, nor is any other war criminal at large," he said.

In a press conference on Wednesday, Mamdani answered more questions about his position and said, "my interest in executing that warrant is not specific to Benjamin Netanyahu, but to anyone who they put a warrant towards for war crimes and crimes against humanity."

Mamdani was asked at one point what he would say to those who are disappointed that Netanyahu can't be arrested by authorities in New York City.

"I'm simply saying that this is a city where, if anyone feels a level of frustration or opposition, protest is something that we will always respect," Mamdani said. "I leave it to New Yorkers themselves to make that decision and when it comes to Palestinian New Yorkers who are disappointed, I understand it because we are talking about someone who has been charged with crimes against humanity, someone who has visited this city on many an occasion and who has be welcomed by previous mayor and I want to make it very clear that that welcome ends with me."

Mamdani continued. "I will not be welcoming Benjamin Netanyahu or any other war criminals when they come to this city, and I do believe that our federal government should join the International Criminal Court and ensure that there is accountability when it comes to crimes against humanity."

Fox News Digital’s Michael Sinkewicz contributed to this report.

item.title: Phillies star JT Realmuto makes inexplicable base-running blunder to end the game, leaves manager dumbfounded
item.description: Don Mattingly said he wasn't sure what J.T. Realmuto was thinking after the catcher's ninth-inning baserunning gaffe sealed a chaotic double play.
item.content:encoded:
Every once in a while, a big leaguer looks like a little leaguer on the diamond.

Unfortunately for the Philadelphia Phillies, star catcher J.T. Realmuto’s Little League-esque blunder ended the game.

In the bottom of the ninth inning of the Phillies’ 2-1 loss to the Los Angeles Dodgers on Tuesday at Citizens Bank Park, the Phillies had runners on second and third with one out.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

Justin Crawford, who pinch-ran for Alec Bohm, was on third base, while Realmuto was on second base. Right fielder Brandon Marsh was up against closer Tanner Scott.

Marsh hit a weak ground ball to third base, and Crawford started to go home but stopped after a few steps.

Interim Phillies manager Don Mattingly said postgame the contact play was on, which means Crawford was supposed to run home no matter what. However, Crawford said the contact play was on unless the ball was hit to third base.

So Crawford, now caught between third base and home plate, got in a rundown. Realmuto ran to third base but inexplicably turned around to go back to second base when he saw Crawford run back to third base.

ANGELS THIRD BASEMAN BRINGS BASKETBALL ONTO THE DIAMOND, TURNS SLICK DOUBLE PLAY WITH UNCONVENTIONAL MOVE

Mookie Betts tagged Crawford out and then whirled and fired to second baseman Tommy Edman to nab Realmuto going back to second, completing a chaotic 5-2-6-4 double play to end the game.

Phillies announcer John Kruk was in disbelief after seeing Realmuto run back toward second base.

"Oh no, JT! Oh no," Kruk said.

Realmuto said postgame it was a bad baserunning play.

"Just a bad baserunning play, really," Realmuto told reporters postgame. "We were going on seeing it through there, and once (Crawford) gets in the rundown and I'm on third base, there’s no reason for me to go back to second. Just a lack of judgment. You can't make two outs on that play. I’ve just got to stay in scoring position."

ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!

"Just a lapse in judgment. I thought Crawford could get out of the rundown for some reason. The chances of that are so small, you just can't take that chance there of making two outs on one play."

Realmuto said he should have played it through in his head before it happened and been more proactive in his preparation. Mattingly said he wasn’t sure what his star catcher was thinking.

"I don’t know what JT was thinking there, honestly, because he got to third," Mattingly told reporters postgame.

"With contact on," Mattingly said, "just hoping (Crawford) stays in (the rundown) long enough to get Marsh to second, and you end up in the same spot."

"I’m not quite sure what he’s thinking. I don’t know if he thought that (Crawford) was going to get back or they were going to let him get back. It’s something we’ll talk about."

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

The baserunning blunder comes in the midst of a down season for Realmuto. The three-time All-Star catcher is hitting just .215 with seven home runs, which well below his career batting average of .268.

The Phillies (56-46) will look to bounce back from the tough loss and take the third game of the three-game set against the Dodgers (64-38) on Wednesday at 6:40 p.m. ET.

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.
